description: Tooling to keep the aim graph healthy as it accumulates cruft over long autonomous-development runs: duplicates, resolved-but-uncollapsed chains, status drift from the actual code, and vague catch-all parents. The reorganization of subtrees (e.g. the 'UI' catch-all, see 18263fc2) should become a byproduct of this tooling rather than a manual slog. Evidence motivating this: 2a9e3aba and f592b156 are byte-identical duplicates; a05ea545/891e2ee0/855e26a1 are three aims for one shipped feature; 8ae9e8da/fe0e0aaf/b3f8518f/4e50493a were all already implemented but still marked open.

status.comment: MCP foundation laid: check_consistency, fix_consistency, build_search_index, search_aims_semantic, list_aims all wired to live MCP server (were backend-only before). Remaining: merge-aim operation (338c12e5), status reconciliation (7625d5e6), hygiene report extension (d6734796), reparent suggester (b0fb3242), taxonomy convention (c69ab6f7).
